back: A programming language used to create interactive web pages.
A container used to store data values.
let is block-scoped, var is function-scoped.
A variable that cannot be reassigned after declaration.
String, Number, Boolean, Null, Undefined, Object, Symbol, BigInt.
A reusable block of code that performs a task.
A shorter syntax for writing functions using =>.
A structure used to store multiple values in a single variable.
A collection of key-value pairs.
=== checks value and type, == only checks value after type conversion.
Document Object Model, a structure representing HTML elements.
An action like click, input, or scroll detected by JavaScript.
Attaches an event handler to an element.
A way to repeat code multiple times.
A loop used when the number of iterations is known.
A loop that runs as long as a condition is true.
An object representing the result of an asynchronous operation.
A syntax to handle Promises in a cleaner, synchronous-looking way.
Prints output to the browser console.
A value that represents 'Not a Number'.
